CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100));
SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'test%');
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'updated_test' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name = (SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id = 1);
BEGIN; SELECT * FROM test_table; LOCK TABLE test_table IN EXCLUSIVE MODE;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name = (SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table));
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, ref_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT a.id, t.name FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON a.ref_id = t.id WHERE t.name LIKE 'updated%';
